qualifying_org_definition: Nonprofit organization (any); no MOU or provider pre-registration required. Local MDHHS offices 'may' maintain a suggested list of willing sites but use is optional. DHS-1997 completed by any nonprofit or government organization.
verification_instrument: DHS-1997 (Community Service Activity Report) — generated monthly by the Bridges eligibility system after individual selects SICS intent; nonprofit representative signs to certify hours worked.
hours_formula_detail: SICS pathway: monthly FAP benefit ÷ Michigan state minimum wage ($13.73 in 2026). BEM 620 example: $292 benefit / $13.73 = 21.26 hours → 21 hours (round down). General volunteer/employment lane remains 80 hrs/month. SICS hours are pre-calculated by Bridges and pre-populated on the DHS-1997.
accepts_outofstate_detail: BEM 620 (BPB 2026-008) says 'unpaid work for a nonprofit organization' with no geographic restriction on where the nonprofit is incorporated or located. Local MDHHS offices may maintain suggested lists of willing sites, but use of such lists is optional. No Michigan policy language requires in-state registration or presence. Federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies absent state restriction.
geographic_waivers: 15 counties and 6 cities waived from TLFA effective December 1, 2025. Waived counties: Alcona, Alger, Arenac, Cheboygan, Iosco, Iron, Luce, Mackinac, Montmorency, Oceana, Ogemaw, Oscoda, Presque Isle, Roscommon, Schoolcraft. Waived cities: Bay City, Detroit, Eastpointe, Flint, Jackson, Saginaw. All other Michigan areas fully enforced. (OBBBA eliminated most prior statewide waivers; these area-specific exemptions were approved under the new post-OBBBA framework.)
submission_deadline: By end of the benefit month. DHS-1997 must be returned to MDHHS caseworker/specialist by month-end to avoid a countable month. BEM 620 example shows submitting on November 3rd for October = countable month (though correctable via late-entry process). MI Bridges upload portal listed as a document upload option for 'Community Service Activity Report'.
notes: Michigan's TLFA (ABAWD) policy has two volunteer pathways: (1) general 'employment' lane includes unpaid volunteer work at 80 hrs/month with no nonprofit-specific form, and (2) Self-Initiated Community Service (SICS) lane where the individual must first indicate SICS intent in the state Bridges system — which then auto-generates the DHS-1997 monthly for the nonprofit to sign — making it a required system-mediated prestep before the form is issued. No MOU or provider registration is required of the nonprofit itself, but the individual must interact with MDHHS/Bridges to trigger the DHS-1997 before the nonprofit can certify hours. county_variation_flag=true because numerous counties (Alcona, Alger, Arenac, etc.) and several cities (Detroit, Flint, Saginaw, etc.) are fully waived from TLFA effective December 1, 2025, making enforcement partial statewide. EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: SICS pathway uses workfare formula (FAP benefit ÷ state minimum wage of $13.73 in 2026), yielding ~21 hrs/month for a representative single-member case ($292 benefit); general volunteer lane remains 80 hrs/month. monthly_hours_required set to 21 as the operative SICS figure since that is the pathway Tended would use. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: BEM 620 says 'nonprofit organization' with no geographic restriction; policy is silent on in-state requirement; federal default applies. (3) Geographic waivers: 15 counties + 6 cities waived under post-OBBBA framework effective Dec 1, 2025; rest of state fully enforced. (4) Submission UX: DHS-1997 is Bridges-generated and returned by recipient to MDHHS caseworker; MI Bridges upload portal supports 'Community Service Activity Report' as document type (mobile_upload). Reporting cadence is monthly. Deadline is by end of the benefit month. (5) Form fields: DHS-1997 is pre-populated by Bridges with recipient name, case number, and required SICS hours; nonprofit representative signs to certify hours. Inferred required fields: recipient_name, recipient_case_number, org_name, org_signature, supervisor_name, hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date.
